behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment, not just local parsing. A stubbed model can be forced to emit the expected signature and would hide real mismatches (MOCK-HIDE). No real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond the model API call, and this is a single model decision, so Agentic (not System/Workflow). Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text only requires signature correctness for a generation, not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real generation from natural-language spec and assert parsed signature exactly matches expected parameter names/types/return type.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestrator control-flow logic that can be reproduced by calling one local orchestrator function with deterministic fakes for generator/test runner (MOCK-MISS down to Unit). No real infra required and no dependence on model output quality; only pass/fail signals matter. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is about ordering/early-return logic itself, which can fail within one function without real module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Call orchestrator with scripted fake outcomes (fail, then pass) and assert exact call order and that third attempt is never invoked.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires sequential agentic steps: generate -> test -> refine -> regenerate, with success emerging from iteration (MULTI-STEP yes). Correctness depends on real LLM output changes across attempts (LLM-DEP yes), so stubs would miss real failure modes. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires the live iterative loop across attempts, not a single model call.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real multi-attempt loop on a known prompt where first attempt commonly fails; verify a later attempt passes within max attempts.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff: test-result structure must be propagated into prompt-construction and then into next request payload (MOCK-MISS across local modules). In-process wiring is sufficient; no real OS/network/subprocess needed (not System). No dependence on model output quality (not Agentic). Rejection gate: rejected Unit because behavior text targets inclusion in the next LLM prompt after test failure, which requires wiring between executor output and prompt request construction.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + test-result adapter + prompt builder with deterministic fakes; induce failure and assert next prompt payload contains exact failed test names/messages.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Requires at least two sequential model decisions with context carryover (first code, failure feedback, next code), so MULTI-STEP applies. Correctness depends on real LLM behavior in using failure context (LLM-DEP yes); deterministic stubs can falsely pass or miss repetition failure. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text is explicitly about the next attempt changing based on prior context, i.e., cross-step agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to failure, feed real failure context into attempt 2, and assert attempt-2 code materially differs and reflects failure-informed adjustments.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This validates cross-module bookkeeping and aggregation across repeated generator/test-runner interactions, best caught with wired in-process components. No real infra required (not System) and no model-quality dependence (deterministic fail stubs sufficient, so not Agentic). Rejection gate: rejected Unit because behavior text requires accumulated per-attempt test results across looped interactions, which is a module orchestration boundary rather than isolated single-function logic in most implementations.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic failing stubs for generation/test execution through three attempts; assert termination at attempt 3 and structured error includes all attempt artifacts/results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets end-to-end local module wiring with deterministic stubs, which is Integration by definition. No real OS/network/subprocess needed and no real model-output-quality assertion. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text says non-agentic with deterministic stubs; rejected Agentic/Workflow for same reason.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline with deterministic stub model outputs and stubbed test outcomes; verify calls, state transitions, and final result are correct end to end.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step live agent loop with sequential model decisions and feedback propagation (MULTI-STEP yes). Success criterion depends on real LLM output quality improving across iterations (LLM-DEP yes), so stubs would hide real failures. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text requires full loop sequence (generate, test, refine, retest), not a single agentic step.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real generate-test-refine-retest loop on a curated hard task known to fail first attempt; assert eventual pass within attempt budget.
